figures.caption: Fig. 2: Ablation of averaging for FG-SFDQN. Curves plot cumulative reward collected on the active task over training steps. FG-SFDQN (Alg. 1) achieves much higher cumulative returns compared to its averaging variants with N = 5, 10, 20.

Fig. 3: Final evaluation performance across environments. FG-SFDQN (Alg. 1) consistently achieves higher returns compared to all baselines. Color scheme: DQN (blue), SFDQN (orange), FG-SFDQN (green), FG-SFDQN Alg. 2 (red), FG-SFDQN Alg. 3 (purple), FGDQN (brown).

Fig. 1: Cumulative training reward across tasks. FG-SFDQN consistently achieves faster learning and higher cumulative returns than baselines across all environments. Resets to zero indicate task transitions.
